title: hemionitis chlorophylla
intro: Hemionitis chlorophylla, also known as the golden spleenwort or the green spleenwort, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Hemionitis chlorophylla is often prepared as an herbal tea or infused oil, with the latter being used topically for skin issues and the former being consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
